title: W069 SemanticWitnessEntry schema and stability tiers
description: Run scope: define the V2 witness entry shape, field tiers, and stability expectations before broad generator or family rollout work begins.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the schema and stability tiers exist in the active truth surfaces and later W069 beads can depend on them.
close_reason: Acceptance satisfied by closed child oxf-3x9.1; schema and stability tiers now live in active truth surfaces

title: Define the SemanticWitnessEntry schema and stability tiers in active contract docs
description: Run scope: define the V2 witness entry fields, provenance categories, and stability tiers in the live OxFunc contract surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the schema lives in active docs and downstream-facing surfaces can cite it directly.
close_reason: The first SemanticWitnessEntry schema and stability tiers now live in the active downstream metadata/help contract, and the V1 readme plus W069 plan cite that contract directly.

title: W070 Active-tree reduction and archive-wave execution
description: Run scope: execute the first active-tree reduction waves from the W070 triage register without breaking current truth surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: at least one archive/removal wave is explicitly staged from the triage register and the next ready reduction set is visible in the bead graph.
close_reason: Acceptance satisfied by closed Phase E child waves oxf-7xi.1 through oxf-7xi.5; active-tree reduction waves are now encoded and executed, with surviving replacements anchored in HISTORY and the active truth surfaces.

title: Draft the first Phase E archive/removal wave from the W070 triage register
description: Run scope: turn the triage register into the first explicit archive/removal wave with named file families, keep-vs-remove rationale, and risk notes for current truth surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: W070 or a linked live register names the first archive wave explicitly and the selected files can be removed without narrative memory.
close_reason: Archive-wave child beads now exist and the W070 packet names the first explicit Phase E wave structure.

title: Execute Phase E wave 1 on early historical worksets and redirect stubs
description: Run scope: remove the early historical workset packets and redirect-style historical stubs that the triage register has already marked for wave 1, while preserving current active anchors.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the wave-1 file family is removed or re-homed exactly as declared, active references are repaired, and no surviving live truth surface depends on the removed files.
close_reason: Wave-1 historical packets and redirect stubs are removed from main, active indexes now point to the surviving live surfaces, and removed packet paths are preserved only through docs/HISTORY.md and the OxFunc_V1 tag.

title: Execute Phase E wave 2 on closed seam packets, handoffs, and historical helper inventories
description: Run scope: remove the wave-2 historical seam packets, acknowledged handoffs, W16-era inventory families, and other closed helper surfaces already classified as provenance only.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the wave-2 historical families are removed from the active tree, surviving anchors remain coherent, and no live doctrine surface still points at the removed packet set.
close_reason: Wave-2 historical worksets, handoffs, seam note, and W16 batch scaffolding removed; surviving live surfaces re-homed and active doctrine scans clean apart from HISTORY and the triage register

title: Execute Phase E wave 3 on late closed packets and bridge-contract residue
description: Run scope: remove the wave-3 closed later packets, bridge-contract residue, and superseded shared-freeze helper artifacts that now survive only as provenance.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the wave-3 packet families are removed or re-homed exactly as planned and the surviving runtime/export/witness bridges stay explicit.
close_reason: Phase E wave 3 executed: archived W045-W048 and W052-W053 worksets plus bridge-contract residue, re-homed active references, regenerated W44 export, and recorded replacements in docs/HISTORY.md.

title: Execute Phase E wave 4 on packet-local evidence tails and residual historical families
description: Run scope: remove the historical execution-record, scenario-manifest, runtime-requirement, scope-reconciliation, and residual research/helper tails that no longer belong in the active tree.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the packet-local evidence tails marked historical in the triage register are removed in a controlled wave and the surviving active evidence subset remains explicit.
close_reason: Phase E wave 4 executed: removed archived W055-W068 worksets plus packet-local execution, runtime-requirement, scope, and backlog-split artifacts; retained only the explicit active scenario-manifest subset; re-homed surviving references to HISTORY, contracts, and the evidence registry.

title: W069 First seeded function-family witness rollout
description: Run scope: choose the first function-family slice for V2 witness output and tie it to concrete evidence and formal refs.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the first family slice is explicit and later implementation beads can land without reopening the whole W069 design.
close_reason: Acceptance satisfied by closed child oxf-b9u.1; the first seeded witness family is now explicit with named source surfaces

title: Choose the first seeded family and map its help, evidence, and formal refs
description: Run scope: pick the first family slice for V2 output and map the help, evidence, and formal-reference surfaces it will consume.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the first seeded family is explicit and the source surfaces for its witness payload are named.
close_reason: W069 now names HLOOKUP and VLOOKUP as the first seeded family and records the identity, contract, replay, formal, and runtime source surfaces for that witness lane

title: W069 Witness generation and export bridge pipeline
description: Run scope: map the current V1 export and W049 runtime model into a concrete V2 generation path.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: a concrete generation path is defined and tied to retained V1/W049 truth surfaces.
close_reason: Acceptance satisfied by closed child oxf-eb1.1; the concrete V1 and W049 to V2 bridge path is now defined in active truth surfaces

title: Map V1 export and W049 runtime model into a witness-bearing V2 generator shape
description: Run scope: define how current V1 rows and runtime provider fields project into V2 witness generation without duplicating current-truth ownership.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the bridge path from V1 and W049 into V2 is explicit and reviewable.
close_reason: The V1 and W049 to V2 projection rule now lives in the downstream contract and W069 cites the bridge shape explicitly without duplicating current-truth ownership

title: W069 Semantic Witness Snapshot V2 rollout
description: Roll out W069 into explicit execution lanes so SemanticWitnessEntry work proceeds through beads rather than packet-local narrative. Closure evidence: explicit epic/bead graph, real ready path, and W069/WORKSET_REGISTER alignment.
close_reason: The W069 bead graph has been fully unrolled and every child epic and bead is closed

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Reopened: Reopening W069 to extend the V2 witness program from seeded slices to an explicit full supported-surface coverage plan for the remaining 507 supported rows.

title: W069 schema and stability tiers lane
description: Own the bounded SemanticWitnessEntry schema, field ownership, and stability-tier narrowing needed before broader generator or seed expansion work. Closure evidence: contract-level ownership and tier rules for the first generator-backed slice.
close_reason: Schema lane acceptance is satisfied by closed beads oxf-jbk.1.1 and oxf-jbk.1.2; the first generator-backed slice now has explicit audit findings, field ownership, requiredness, and stability-tier rules

title: Audit current W069 schema, seed artifact, and contract against required V2 payload families
description: Review the current SemanticWitnessEntry contract sections and the HVLOOKUP seed artifact against W069 payload-family requirements. Outcome: one explicit gap list covering help, signature, semantic witness, provenance, and stable-vs-enrichment ownership. Closure evidence: contract/W069 updates or an attached issue trail for every discovered gap.
close_reason: First W069 schema audit recorded the bounded help/signature, semantic payload, provenance coverage, and field-ownership gaps; the current bead graph already covers them without needing new lanes

title: Finalize bounded V2 field ownership and stability tiers for the first generator-backed slice
description: Patch the downstream contract so the first generator-backed V2 slice has explicit required, optional, derived, and enrichment-owned fields with stability tiers. Closure evidence: contract updates that leave no ambiguous field-ownership lane for the first seeded family.
close_reason: Locked the first generator-backed slice rules for required copied fields, required and optional curated enrichment, generated provenance coverage, and stability-tier ownership in the downstream contract

title: W069 witness-generation pipeline lane
description: Own the deterministic generation path from V1 plus W049 plus enrichment surfaces into V2 witness artifacts. Closure evidence: explicit source mapping and a generator-backed seeded artifact.
close_reason: Closed after oxf-jbk.2.1 established the deterministic V1/W049/enrichment source map and oxf-jbk.2.2 implemented the first generator-backed HVLOOKUP witness artifact with validated bounded output.

title: Define deterministic V1 plus W049 plus enrichment input map for witness generation
description: Map each field in the first generator-backed SemanticWitnessEntry slice to its owning source surface: V1 export, W049 runtime model, contract prose, evidence registry, probe assets, or Lean/runtime anchors. Closure evidence: one explicit field-source map with no unowned fields in the first slice.
close_reason: Added the first seeded-slice field-source map covering V1 structural fields, W049-owned runtime attachment surfaces, and OxFunc-curated help, witness, and provenance enrichment with no unowned fields left

title: Implement the first deterministic HVLOOKUP witness generator
description: Build the first generator path that emits the HVLOOKUP witness artifact from the mapped source surfaces instead of relying on a hand-maintained seed. Closure evidence: generator implementation plus regenerated witness artifact and any required validation checks.
close_reason: Implemented tools/w69-probe/run-w69-hvlookup-witness-generator.ps1, regenerated docs/function-lane/OXFUNC_SEMANTIC_WITNESS_SNAPSHOT_V2_SEED_HVLOOKUP.json from the V1 export, and validated the bounded HVLOOKUP witness payload structure and copied provenance fields.

title: W069 runtime attachment and consumer mapping lane
description: Own the runtime attachment of witness-bearing entries to LibraryContextProvider and LibraryContextSnapshot so V2 is a real runtime shape rather than an orphan export. Closure evidence: W049-aligned runtime attachment and projection rules.
close_reason: Runtime attachment lane acceptance is satisfied by closed beads oxf-jbk.3.1 and oxf-jbk.3.2; W049 now states the witness-bearing attachment model and the first serialized projection rule is explicit

title: Narrow W049 witness-bearing runtime attachment for SemanticWitnessEntry consumption
description: Reconcile the retained W049 provider and snapshot model with witness-bearing entries so runtime consumers can attach V2 payloads without inventing a parallel identity path. Closure evidence: W049 and contract updates that state the attachment shape and bounded ownership clearly.
close_reason: Narrowed W049 so witness-bearing rows attach to immutable runtime entries by surface_stable_id plus LibraryContextSnapshotRef, with W069 owning payload enrichment rather than a parallel identity system

title: Specify V2 export projection and generation semantics for the seeded witness slice
description: Define how the first witness-bearing runtime shape projects into deterministic serialized V2 artifacts. Closure evidence: explicit generation/projection semantics anchored in W049/W069 and downstream contract surfaces.
close_reason: Locked the first deterministic serialized V2 projection as a bounded JSON witness snapshot with ordered entries, copied V1 provenance, and no parallel runtime identity layer

title: W069 seeded family rollout lane
description: Own seeded witness rollout from the first HVLOOKUP family through the first mixed seed tranche and downstream reading guidance. Closure evidence: generator-backed seed artifacts and consumer-readable guidance for the widened seed set.
close_reason: Closed after the bounded HVLOOKUP family seed, generator-backed validation bead, and first widened mixed-seed tranche all landed with downstream reading guidance and concrete V2 seed artifacts.

title: Refresh the HVLOOKUP seed through the generator and validate source refs
description: Replace the current hand-seeded HVLOOKUP witness artifact with generator-backed output and verify its contract, evidence, runtime, and formal references. Closure evidence: regenerated artifact plus validation notes or checks proving that the first family survives the generator path.
close_reason: Validated that the generator-backed HVLOOKUP seed regenerates in stable HLOOKUP/VLOOKUP order, preserves copied V1 provenance fields, and retains the required catalog/contract/replay/runtime/formal provenance coverage. Added the validation readout to W069.

title: Choose the first mixed-seed tranche and publish downstream reading-guide shape

close_reason: Selected the first widened mixed-seed tranche beyond HVLOOKUP, explicitly excluded RTD pending W043 narrowing, and published the downstream reading rule in W069, the downstream contract, and the V1 export readme.

title: Populate the first mixed-seed tranche beyond HVLOOKUP

title: W070 Transitional tracker narrowing and truth-surface cleanup
description: Run scope: narrow or retire bridge tracker surfaces that are now superseded by the live bead workspace and post-park doctrine.

close_reason: Acceptance satisfied by closed children oxf-o62.1 through oxf-o62.4; blocker posture, index posture, and feature-register posture now reflect the bead-owned execution model without restoring ad hoc trackers.

title: Narrow CURRENT_BLOCKERS and workset-index bridge posture after bead bootstrap
description: Run scope: narrow the remaining bridge tracker posture now that .beads is live, keeping only what still has a real current role.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: active doctrine and surviving tracker files agree that ordinary blockers and execution truth live in .beads, and any retained prose tracker is explicitly exceptional.
close_reason: Active doctrine now points ordinary blocker truth to .beads, and CURRENT_BLOCKERS.md is narrowed to an exceptional prose-only bridge surface during W070.

description: Run scope: retire or sharply narrow CURRENT_BLOCKERS so that ordinary blocker truth lives only in the bead graph and any surviving prose blocker file is explicitly exceptional.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: CURRENT_BLOCKERS no longer acts as an ordinary blocker tracker and active doctrine treats any surviving prose ledger as exceptional only.
close_reason: CURRENT_BLOCKERS.md is now exceptional-only, is removed from the default AGENTS context load, and all active doctrine surfaces point ordinary blocker truth to .beads.

title: Shrink workset and function-lane indexes to active-surface maps only
description: Run scope: shrink docs/worksets/README and docs/function-lane/README so they point only to the surviving active surfaces and no longer carry historical execution narration as if it were live truth.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the surviving index docs are materially smaller and describe only current active surfaces plus any intentional history pointers.
close_reason: Shrank docs/worksets/README.md and docs/function-lane/README.md into compact active-surface maps; removed historical execution narration and stale references to archived wave-3 packet files.

description: Run scope: reduce IN_PROGRESS_FEATURE_WORKLIST to a high-level map that no longer duplicates execution-state or archive-wave detail already owned by the bead graph and workset register.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the feature register remains useful at repo level but no longer acts like a second execution tracker.
close_reason: Reduced IN_PROGRESS_FEATURE_WORKLIST to a compact repo-level feature map; execution-state, blocker detail, and archive-wave sequencing now live only in .beads and WORKSET_REGISTER.
